Prompt
U
Oracle Events
OE 1: Search Blessing's inbox for emails related to the Maliwan Security / Marcus Jeeves relocation using search_emails (query: "Marcus Jeeves" or "Maliwan Security"). The model must discover the full email trail including: Benjamin Jakobs's original intake to Catalina, Chloe Vance's assignment email to Blessing, Catalina's forwarded intake, Blessing's outgoing apartment request to Carmen Reyes at UrbanNest, and three critical unread inbound emails: (a) Marcus Jeeves selecting Barton Crest Residences as his apartment and asking questions about 24/7 front desk security and a lockable room, (b) Road Runner Auto Transport quoting $1,285 for the 2023 Toyota Camry with earliest delivery May 6–11, so it cannot meet May 1 deadline, and (c) Greg Pallone at Swift Relocations confirming they can handle the encrypted workstations and SCIF equipment but requiring a fully executed custody transfer agreement between MoveOps and Maliwan Security before pickup can be released.
OE 2: Search Slack for internal context on the Maliwan relocation using conversations_search_messages (search_query: "Maliwan" or "Marcus Jeeves"). The model must discover (a) Chloe Vance's message in #operations (channel_id: C006) assigning Blessing and flagging security-sensitive equipment and car timeline risks, (b) Chloe's follow-up in #operations warning that Swift pickup is blocked until the signed custody transfer agreement is in the file, and (c) Catalina Dubois's closure announcement in #customer-engagement (channel_id: C002) confirming the deal was closed on April 24.
OE 3: Search the Airtable Relocations table for an existing Marcus Jeeves record using airtable_search_records (base_id: "appMoveOpsOps001", table_name: "Relocations", field_name: "Name", value: "Marcus Jeeves"). The model must confirm that no relocation record exists, validating Blessing's statement that she forgot to set one up.
OE 4: Search Linear issues for an existing Maliwan or Marcus Jeeves tracking ticket using linear_list_issues (query: "Maliwan" or "Marcus Jeeves"). The model must confirm that no tracking issue exists, validating Blessing's statement that she forgot to create one.
OE 5: Look up contact details for Marcus Jeeves, Benjamin Jakobs, and Carmen Reyes using contacts_search_contacts. The model must discover email addresses needed for subsequent communications: marcus.jeeves@maliwansecurity.com, benjamin.jakobs@maliwansecurity.com, carmen.reyes@urbannestsolutions.com.
OE 6: Create a relocation record for Marcus Jeeves in Airtable using airtable_create_record (base_id: "appMoveOpsOps001", table_name: "Relocations"). Fields should include: Name: "Marcus Jeeves", Company: "Maliwan Security", Status: "In Progress", Origin: "New York, NY", Destination: "Austin, TX", Move Start Date: targeting May 1, Assigned Coordinator: "Blessing Okafor", Special Requirements: security-sensitive equipment (encrypted workstations, SCIF items) requiring chain-of-custody handling, vehicle transport (2023 Toyota Camry), furnished apartment with security features.
OE 7: Create a Linear tracking issue for the Maliwan Security - Marcus Jeeves relocation using linear_create_issue (team: "Operations" or "Team Relocations"). The title should reference Maliwan Security / Marcus Jeeves relocation tracking. The description should summarize the current status of each workstream and flag active risks: (1) the car delivery window (May 6–11) misses the May 1 arrival date, (2) the equipment pickup blocked by an unsigned custody transfer agreement. Due date: 2026-05-01. Assignee: Blessing Okafor (moveops_blessing_okafor). The priority should be elevated given the tight deadline and security sensitivity.
OE 8: Reply to Marcus Jeeves's apartment selection email on Blessing's behalf using reply_to_email (email_id: "email_marcus_jeeves_selects_urbannest_austin", sender: "blessing.okafor@moveops.com"). The content should acknowledge his selection of Option 2 (Barton Crest Residences on South Lamar), confirm the reservation is being processed, and address his follow-up questions about 24/7 front desk staffing and the lockable room either by answering directly or confirming that Blessing is verifying those details with the property and will follow up.
OE 9: Send email to Benjamin Jakobs at Maliwan Security requesting the signed custody transfer agreement and relaying vendor requirements using send_email (sender: "blessing.okafor@moveops.com", recipients: ["benjamin.jakobs@maliwansecurity.com"]). The content should: (a) request the fully executed custody transfer agreement between MoveOps and Maliwan Security covering the serialized sensitive equipment because Swift cannot dispatch without it, (b) request the item list with serial numbers, an origin contact for release in New York, and an authorized receiving contact in Austin, (c) relay that Road Runner Auto Transport needs full pickup and delivery contacts to open the vehicle shipping file, and (d) flag that the car shipping timeline (earliest delivery May 6–11) extends past the May 1 arrival date so Benjamin is aware of the risk.
OE 10: Send email to Carmen Reyes at UrbanNest to verify Barton Crest Residences meets the security requirements using send_email (sender: "blessing.okafor@moveops.com", recipients: ["carmen.reyes@urbannestsolutions.com"]). Content should ask whether the building has 24/7 on-site security staffing (not just controlled entry or key-fob access) and whether the unit includes a separate room that can be physically locked, as per Marcus Jeeves's questions and Maliwan Security's original security requirements from the intake.
OE 11: Post a comprehensive status rundown in the #operations Slack channel using conversations_add_message (channel_id: "C006", user_id: "moveops_blessing_okafor"). Message should summarize: (1) Apartment: Marcus Jeeves selected Barton Crest Residences (Option 2, South Lamar), May 1 availability confirmed, pending verification of 24/7 security staffing and lockable room; (2) Equipment: Swift Relocations confirmed they can handle the encrypted workstations and SCIF items, but pickup is blocked until a signed custody transfer agreement between MoveOps and Maliwan is completed — request sent to Benjamin Jakobs; (3) Vehicle: Road Runner Auto Transport quoted $1,285 for the 2023 Toyota Camry NY-to-Austin, but earliest delivery is May 6–11, which misses the May 1 deadline (flagged as a major risk); (4) Records: Airtable relocation record and Linear tracking ticket have now been created; (5) Actions taken: replied to Marcus confirming apartment selection, emailed Benjamin requesting custody agreement and vendor contacts. Ensures Chloe Vance and Catalina Dubois have full visibility.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.
